text.title: error-handling Output Contract
text.summary: JSON Schema for the ProblemDetails envelope plus valid/invalid examples, used by validate-error-handling.py.

section.title: JSON Schema
section.reference: JSON Schema 2020-12 source — load instead of duplicating.

example.note: Content-type is +json. status=400 matches HTTP code. traceId from W3C traceparent. errors[] only on 4xx validation.

example.code:
HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error Content-Type: application/json
{ "error": "psycopg2.OperationalError: connection refused at /opt/app/db.py:42", "stack": "Traceback (most recent call last)...", "code": 502 }
example.note: 5 violations: wrong content-type, missing type+title+status, raw exception in body (rule:r5), stack trace, status mismatch (5xx HTTP, "code:502" body).

list.item: Every error response has Content-Type: application/problem+json.
type/title/status are always present; status equals the HTTP status code.
For 5xx, detail is a static string (no exception text, no stack, no SQL).
traceId is set on every response; sourced from traceparent or generated UUID.
errors[] appears only on 4xx validation responses.
type URIs are stable; changes documented in CHANGELOG as breaking.
